mammoth.name: Asian Elephant
mammoth.forText: Closest living relative — Colossal Biosciences already targeting this pair for de-extinction. Genome is well sequenced and deeply annotated.
mammoth.againstText: Cold-adapted gene suite still largely unknown. Significant engineering needed for mammoth-specific traits like fat deposition and fur growth.

mammoth.name: African Bush Elephant
mammoth.forText: Body mass closely matches mammoth profile. Strong ecosystem-engineering behaviour well documented across African biomes.
mammoth.againstText: Greater genomic divergence than Asian elephant. Tropical specialist — cold-adaptation pathway significantly less clear.

nfwolf.name: Grey Wolf
nfwolf.forText: Nearest continental relative. Pack structure and prey specialisation directly analogous to the Newfoundland subspecies. Already proven as a trophic restorer.
nfwolf.againstText: Island isolation shaped a distinct skull morphology and prey preference — not a direct drop-in replacement for the beothucus subspecies.

nfwolf.name: Coyote
nfwolf.forText: Shares Atlantic Canada's coastal ecosystems and is known to rapidly fill trophic gaps left by wolves. Highly plastic predator.
nfwolf.againstText: Trophic mismatch — coyotes are mesopredators. Their prey base doesn't replicate the wolf's role in regulating caribou and moose populations.

dodo.name: Nicobar Pigeon
dodo.forText: Closest living relative of the dodo. Genome provides the best available reference for dodo sequence reconstruction.
dodo.againstText: 20 million years of independent evolution. Very different body plan and size — extensive genomic reconstruction required.

dodo.name: Rock Dove
dodo.forText: Well-studied genome with decades of breeding research. Accessible reference for pigeon-lineage genomic work.
dodo.againstText: Diverged ~25 Mya. Urban generalist with no island frugivore behaviour — seed dispersal ecology entirely absent from its profile.

labduck.name: Common Eider
labduck.forText: Shares the same molluscivore niche along North Atlantic coastlines. Habitat and range overlap extensively with the Lab Duck's historic territory.
labduck.againstText: Diverged ~8 Mya. The Lab Duck's uniquely shaped bill suggests a specialised foraging method not present in eiders.

labduck.name: Surf Scoter
labduck.forText: Native to eastern North America — shares the Labrador Duck's original coastal range and diving foraging strategy.
labduck.againstText: More distant genomically. Freshwater breeding specialist vs the Lab Duck's obligate marine lifestyle.
